title: Launcher IocpPipeRelay buffers stderr by default — visibility needs --stderr or --sudo
desc: Under DETACHED_PROCESS spawn, Core stdin/stdout/stderr go through Launcher IOCP pipes. By default stderr is BUFFERED not passthrough — only shown on non-zero exit. PULSE + [ELEVATION] trails (stderr) are invisible unless --stderr flag or (v5.14.x) --sudo flag is present.
steps:
ARCHITECTURE
Launcher/IocpPipeRelay.cs:
  const ulong KEY_STDOUT = 1, KEY_STDERR = 2;
  Action writeStderr = showStderr
    ? (buf, len) => _stderr.Write(buf, 0, len)     // passthrough
    : (buf, len) => stderrBuf.Add(...);              // buffer only
Default showStderr = false → stderr swallowed silently during normal runs.
Buffer is printed ONLY when Core exit code != 0.

ENABLE PASSTHROUGH
CLI:  wkappbot --stderr <cmd>
Implicit: wkappbot --sudo <cmd>  (v5.14.x auto-enables for tracing visibility)
Env:  WKAPPBOT_STDERR=1 (not implemented, use --stderr flag)

IMPLICATION FOR INSTRUMENTATION
Any Console.Error.WriteLine from Core will vanish under normal --only-core spawn unless --stderr passed.
PulseStep.Line uses Console.Error → same fate.
Design choice: prevents noise in bash pipes, but hides debug trails.

WHY --sudo IMPLICITLY ENABLES IT
--sudo flow produces valuable diagnostic output:
  [PULSE:Main] step-by-step progression
  [ELEVATION] reason + recent trail before UAC
  [SUDO] decision markers
All would be invisible without passthrough — user can't trace admin routing failures.
v5.14.x: showStderr = args.Any(a => a == "--stderr"

TROUBLESHOOTING
If you add Console.Error.WriteLine to Core and don't see output:
  1. Are you passing --stderr? Default is buffered.
  2. Is exit code 0? If yes, buffer is dropped.
  3. Try Console.WriteLine (stdout) — always visible.
  4. Or enable --stderr / --sudo for automatic visibility.
author: claude
